signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7189 following RBA rate hike to 4.35%. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) while retail sentiment is 86% short—a sharp institutional vs. retail divergence. Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35% creates a -60bp carry spread favoring AUD. Institutional length is building momentum into the hike while retail fades the move; retail capitulation risk supports a move toward 0.7250 if upside persists.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) vs RBNZ hawkish bias with a hike priced for the 27 May meeting. Specs are heavily fading a central bank that remains in a tightening bias. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% provides a +150bp USD carry advantage, but crowded spec shorts face squeeze risk if RBNZ confirms the hike path. NZD/USD at 0.5891.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.88 supported by +300bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded. While VIX at 17.2 (normal volatility) keeps carry trades active, 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal warnings or direct intervention. Oil shock headlines introduce JPY weakness via trade balance concerns, offsetting safe-haven bids unless VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.731 vs 252d norm of -0.138 (z=+1.86σ). This significant decoupling suggests EUR is trading as a proxy for European equity beta rather than rate differentials. ECB's Villeroy confirms no immediate signs to raise rates (ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75%), maintaining a +175bp USD carry advantage. EUR/USD holding at 1.1700; watch for a return to negative equity correlation to signal a shift back to macro fundamentals.
